title: Monosomal karyotype AML
question: Which cytogenetic pattern fulfils the definition of a monosomal karyotype?
options: Two or more autosomal monosomies, or one autosomal monosomy plus at least one structural abnormality.
correct_answer: A. Two or more autosomal monosomies, or one autosomal monosomy plus at least one structural abnormality.
explanation: Monosomal karyotype identifies a particularly high-risk subgroup of AML.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
